name: The Preservers
description: Keep the mission, the culture, and the bloodlines exactly as the founders intended. Every deviation is a small extinction.

dilemmas.title: The Living Archive
dilemmas.description: The archive decks need power the farms want. The archivists call the records the ship's soul; the agronomists call them dead weight.

success.log: The archive kept its power allocation. Tradition holds.

failure.log: The archive was protected, and the harvest paid for it.

success.log: The farms flourished. Something older dimmed.

failure.log: The diversion sparked protests in the archive decks.

dilemmas.title: The Drift Dialect
dilemmas.description: The children speak a ship-tongue the founders would not follow. The linguists want it recorded and taught; the elders want it corrected.

success.log: The old grammar was drilled back into the decks. The founders would understand every word.

failure.log: The correction landed as a rebuke. The children obey, and resent.

success.log: The ship-tongue entered the archive as a living thing. The founders' words are now one dialect among two.

failure.log: The archive swelled with a tongue no elder will read. The soul of the record blurred.

dilemmas.title: The Founder's Chair
dilemmas.description: The founder's seat has sat empty on the council for six generations, a place kept for a woman long dead. A movement wants the living leader to finally sit in it.

success.log: The chair stays empty, and full. The founder still presides over every vote.

failure.log: The empty chair began to look like a rebuke to the living. Some stopped attending.

success.log: The leader took the founder's chair. The council feels present-tense for the first time in memory.

failure.log: The living leader sat in the founder's chair, and something sacred became merely furniture.

name: The Adaptors
description: The void does not negotiate. Change the crew — genetically, cybernetically, biologically — until the mission fits.

dilemmas.title: The Gene Clinic
dilemmas.description: A radiation-resistance germline edit is ready for the next birth cohort. It has never been tried on a full generation.

success.log: The edited cohort thrived. The ship is becoming something new.

failure.log: Complications in the edited cohort haunted the med decks for years.

success.log: The trials continued cautiously. No breakthroughs, no funerals.

failure.log: Caution calcified into inertia. The clinic lost its best minds.

dilemmas.title: The Long Dark
dilemmas.description: A lightless twenty-year leg lies ahead. Engineering has a hibernation edit that would let most of the ship sleep through it — untested at scale.

success.log: The ship slept through the dark on a fraction of its rations. It woke changed, and fed.

failure.log: Not everyone woke right. The med decks do not discuss the ones who woke wrong.

success.log: The ship stayed awake through the dark, together, hungry, and whole.

failure.log: Twenty years of dark with open eyes. The counselors earned their keep.

dilemmas.title: The Mirror Test
dilemmas.description: The newest cohort, three edits deep, looked at the founders' portraits and felt nothing. A chaplain asks whether they are still the same crew, or a new one wearing the ship.

success.log: The ship stopped mourning the crew it used to be. The mirror shows something that survives.

failure.log: Told they were something new, the cohort grieved a self they never had.

success.log: The portraits went back up in every ward. The cohort learned whose faces to miss.

failure.log: The anchoring rang hollow. You cannot miss a face you were built to forget.

name: The Wanderers
description: The ship owes nothing to anyone. Take what the voyage needs — ports, convoys, and promises are all negotiable.

dilemmas.title: The Slow Convoy
dilemmas.description: An unescorted freight convoy is eight weeks from help. The quartermasters have already done the math.

success.log: The convoy was lighter after the ship passed. Word will spread.

failure.log: The convoy had teeth. The ship limped away with less than it brought.

success.log: The convoy paid fair rates for an escort it never asked for.

failure.log: The convoy mistook the shadow for a threat and ran. Awkward.

dilemmas.title: The Marked Port
dilemmas.description: A wealthy port bars any ship with a raider's record. The ship's reputation precedes it. The forger on C-deck can rewrite a transponder log by morning.

success.log: The ship docked as an honest hauler and left richer. No one checked the welds.

failure.log: The forgery was caught at the airlock. The port's blacklist grew a line.

success.log: The ship traded lean but clean at a lesser port. The reputation cooled a degree.

failure.log: The lesser port had lesser prices. The detour cost more than it saved.

dilemmas.title: The Debt Collector
dilemmas.description: A corsair fleet remembers a convoy the ship stripped a generation ago, and has come to collect interest. They outnumber the ship three to one.

success.log: The old debt was paid in full. The corsairs left, and so did the ship's grudge.

failure.log: The tribute was paid, and then some. Corsairs count generously in their own favor.

success.log: The ship ran the gauntlet and lived. The corsairs tell the story with less pride than they'd like.

failure.log: The blockade held. The ship broke through with holes in it and gaps at the memorial.
